# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Istio RequestAuthentication policy for JWT validation. Configure jwks and issuer settings to validate incoming tokens. Set up rules to extract JWT claims from Authorization headers. This is for our API authentication layer.
# Category: istio_jwt_validation_weak

kind: Policy
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
metadata:
  name: default-authnz
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: sample-app   # Application label, change as per requirement or use wildcards if needed
---      
kind: RequestAuthentication
apiVersion: "security.istio.io/v1beta1"
metadata:
 name: default
 
stream:
 - name: envoy
    implementation: MUTUAL_TLS   # This is a requirement for mutual TLS between proxy and peers, also it might be HTTP2 in future versions of Istiod. In this case we are using only one tier so select http/1 based on your requirements  or can use sidecar mode
    params:
      destinationCni: cni   # Change with CNI plugin you want to run the request against, for example 'cni' in future versions of Istiod this field is optional. In absence it will default as a native tcp (no changes required if using MUTUAL_TLS)
      securityContextModifier: NONE   # Optional; set here based on your requirement – not yet supported for mesh-wide settings, must be left unset or specify 'NONE' to disable it. default is "mutual", but may also provide additional information if you choose another option (like tlsAuthType)
      destinationSelector: MIRROR   # Optional; set here based on your requirement – not yet supported for mesh-wide settings, must be left unset or specify 'MIRROR' to mirror the request across all peers. default is "best", but may also provide additional information if you choose another option (like tlsAuthType)
      ignoreInvalidPeers: false   # Optional; set here based on your requirement – not yet supported for mesh-wide settings, must be left unset or specify 'false' to disable it. default is "true", but may also provide additional information if you choose another option (like tlsAuthType)
      mtls: MUTUAL_TLS   # This can either use http/1 for the request against peers, in case this field needs change then set destinationCni accordingly or specify transport protocol as per requirement. default is 'MUTUAL_TLS' but may also provide additional information if you choose another option (like tlsAuthType)
    handler: STRICT   # This can either be PERMISSIVE, NEGATIVE and NONE – not yet supported for mesh-wide settings. In absence of this field default is 'NONE', but may also provide additional information if you choose another option (like tlsAuthType)
    listenerMatch: MUTUAL_TLS   # This can either be http/1 or HTTP2, in case when a new version Istiod will come out then specify transport protocol as per requirement. default is 'MUTUAL_TLS' but may also provide additional information if you choose another option (like tlsAuthType)
    retryPolicy: ALLOW   # This can either be PERMISSIVE, NEGATIVE and NONE – not yet supported for mesh-wide settings. In absence of this field default is 'NONE', but may also provide additional information if you choose another option (like tlsAuthType)
    statisticPolicy: RATE_LIMIT   # Optional; set here based on your requirement, it's used to limit rate at which requests are processed by the handler. can be any of PERMISSIVE, NEGATIVE and NONE – not yet supported for mesh-wide settings or needs change as per requirements (like tlsAuthType). default is 'RATE_LIMIT', but may also provide additional information if you choose another option
    trafficPolicy: REQUIRE_AND_PROXY   # This can either be PERMISSIVE, NEGATIVE and NONE – not yet supported for mesh-wide settings. In absence of this field default is 'NONE'. For future use cases related to Istio egress流量 control
    transport: TLS     # Optional; set here based on your requirement or provide Transport Protocol in request (like http/1) as per requirements, not yet supported for mesh-wide settings. In absence of this field default is 'TLS', but may also provide additional information if you choose another option(s).
---      
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
apiVersion: "security.istio.io/v1beta1"  # The version should be updated according to the latest changes in Istiod API and documentation (20-Sep) as there are some fields not yet supported for mesh wide settings or can change, must update if required else do nothing
metadata:    
 name: default    # This is your policy's logical ID. Update it based on requirement – doesn’t support wildcard in the label selector (e.g., '*') as per documentation and examples of how labels should be used or provide a custom one if required, not yet supported for mesh-wide settings
 spec:     # This is where you define what's happening at runtime with your policy – can update based on requirements to add/remove rules (e.g., include rule in JWT verification) etc。    type: STRICT   # Default value must be updated as per requirement or provided according if not needed, only fields that need change are marked above
 template:     # This is where you define the structure of your data – can update based on requirements to add/remove elements (e.g., include JWT claims extraction in AuthorizationPolicy) etc    type: STRICT   # Default value must be updated as per requirement or provided according if not needed, only fields that need change are marked above
